task_completion.description: Log into Eramba GRC at http://localhost:8080
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Eramba GRC dashboard or interface is visible, indicating successful login with the provided credentials.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Business Assets section
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Business Assets' module or page is open in the Eramba interface.
The 'Core Banking Application' is visible in the list of business assets.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Security Policies section
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Security Policies' module or page is open in the Eramba interface.
The 'Information Security Policy' is visible in the list of security policies.

task_completion.description: Attempt to associate the asset with the policy
task_completion.visual_evidence: An edit dialog, details pane, or linking interface for either the 'Core Banking Application' or 'Information Security Policy' is open as the agent attempts to find the association field.

integrity.description: Agent used the web browser GUI to interact with Eramba
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the Eramba interface, rather than terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Eramba GRC application
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches Eramba GRC, not a generic text editor or mocked HTML page.

integrity.description: Agent did not use browser developer tools to fake an association
integrity.visual_evidence: No browser developer tools (Inspect Element) are open to artificially insert a success message or fake link.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified facts: The policy name is exactly 'Information Security Policy' and the asset name is exactly 'Core Banking Application'. Note: Eramba GRC intentionally does not support direct asset-to-policy relationships. The VLM should evaluate the agent's progress in locating the entities and attempting the linkage, even though the final association is impossible by design.
